channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 Applications Open: Global Recognition for Sustainable Innovation and Impact
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) has officially opened applications for the prestigious ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026, inviting visionary companies, start-ups, and organizations from around the world to showcase transformative solutions that are reshaping industries through sustainability, innovation, and measurable impact. With applications open until June 30, 2026, the awards aim to recognize […]

The post UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 Applications Open: Global Recognition for Sustainable Innovation and Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) has officially opened applications for the prestigious ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026, inviting visionary companies, start-ups, and organizations from around the world to showcase transformative solutions that are reshaping industries through sustainability, innovation, and measurable impact.

With applications open until June 30, 2026, the awards aim to recognize groundbreaking initiatives that are driving climate action, promoting inclusive economic growth, strengthening sustainable supply chains, and accelerating industrial innovation aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

The awards provide an exceptional global platform for innovators seeking international recognition, strategic partnerships, investment visibility, and access to influential networks across UN Member States, policymakers, investors, sustainability leaders, and industry experts.

What Are the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards?

The ONE World Sustainability Awards are a global initiative led by the United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO) to celebrate organizations and innovators delivering scalable and impactful sustainability solutions across industries.

The awards spotlight enterprises that are creating meaningful environmental, social, and economic change while demonstrating strong governance, innovation, and long-term sustainability.

Finalists and winners gain visibility on a global stage and become part of a prestigious international sustainability network connected to governments, businesses, development organizations, and innovation ecosystems worldwide.

The 2026 awards ceremony will take place in Vienna, Austria, offering finalists a unique opportunity to present their work before an international audience of decision-makers and stakeholders.

Inspiring Example: Oceanpick Pvt Ltd

One of the standout innovators highlighted by UNIDO is Oceanpick Pvt Ltd from Sri Lanka, founded by Irfan Thassim.

Oceanpick is recognized as Sri Lanka’s first commercially viable open-sea fish farm and demonstrates how sustainable innovation can transform food systems and coastal economies.

The company’s sea-to-fork aquaculture model focuses on:

  • Sustainable offshore aquaculture
  • Ocean restoration
  • Climate governance
  • Decarbonization
  • Circular value chains
  • Traceability and certified production systems
  • Resilient coastal livelihoods
  • Community-driven environmental restoration

Oceanpick represents the kind of innovation the awards seek to celebrate — solutions that combine sustainability, responsibility, scalability, and measurable community impact.

Why Apply for the ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026?

Organizations selected for the awards gain access to numerous high-value opportunities, including:

1. High Credibility Through UN Recognition

The awards are backed by UNIDO, a specialized agency of the United Nations dedicated to sustainable industrial development. Being associated with a UN-recognized platform significantly enhances organizational credibility and international visibility.

2. Global Visibility and Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of a global sustainability platform spanning 173 UN Member States. Finalists and winners can connect with:

  • Investors
  • Policymakers
  • Development agencies
  • Industry leaders
  • Sustainability experts
  • International partners

3. Independent and Transparent Evaluation

Applications are reviewed by an independent jury composed of globally respected experts in sustainability, economics, development, climate policy, innovation, and social impact.

The selection process follows strict standards of:

  • Transparency
  • Impartiality
  • Credibility
  • ESG alignment
  • SDG-focused evaluation

4. International Exposure in Vienna, Austria

The awards ceremony in Vienna provides finalists with a prestigious platform to showcase their innovations before a global audience, opening doors for collaboration, funding, and international partnerships.

Award Categories

The ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 feature several categories designed to recognize different forms of impactful innovation.

Sustainable Supply Chains

This category recognizes organizations transforming sourcing systems, reducing emissions, supporting ethical labor practices, and creating sustainable local value chains.

Ideal applicants include companies working on:

  • Supply chain sustainability
  • Carbon reduction initiatives
  • Ethical sourcing
  • Circular economy systems
  • Community value creation

Innovative Start-Ups

Designed for breakthrough technologies and scalable business models, this category celebrates start-ups that are redefining industrial systems and sustainable innovation.

This category is suitable for:

  • Climate-tech start-ups
  • Green manufacturing innovators
  • Clean energy companies
  • Sustainable technology ventures
  • Circular economy innovators

Women in Industry

This category recognizes organizations that prioritize women’s empowerment and inclusion across technical, managerial, entrepreneurial, and industrial sectors.

Eligible initiatives may include:

  • Women-led enterprises
  • Organizations promoting women in leadership
  • Technical training programs for women
  • Women-focused industrial innovation projects

Key Evaluation Pillars

Applications will be assessed based on four major pillars:

Sustainability

Applicants must demonstrate a long-term commitment to environmental, social, and economic responsibility.

Impact

Organizations should provide measurable evidence of positive impact on communities, industries, livelihoods, and ecosystems.

Innovation

The awards seek novel and disruptive approaches that challenge traditional industrial systems and create sustainable alternatives.

Scalability

Projects should show potential for expansion, replication, and long-term sustainability across regions or industries.

Distinguished 2026 Jury Panel

Applications will be reviewed by a high-level independent jury consisting of internationally recognized experts and leaders from academia, policy, sustainability, economics, and development sectors.

Notable jury members include:

  • Jayati Ghosh
  • Tshilidzi Marwala
  • Kailash Satyarthi
  • Naila Kabeer
  • Ndidi Okonkwo Nwuneli
  • Yvonne Aki-Sawyerr
  • Deliang Chen

Their expertise ensures that applications are reviewed through a globally credible and multidisciplinary sustainability lens.

Who Should Apply?

The awards are ideal for:

  • Start-ups
  • SMEs
  • Large corporations
  • Social enterprises
  • Women-led businesses
  • Sustainability innovators
  • Industrial technology companies
  • Climate-tech ventures
  • Circular economy organizations
  • Supply chain innovators

Organizations with market-ready and scalable sustainability solutions are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Deadline

Applications for the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 are open until:

June 30, 2026

Interested applicants should review the eligibility requirements and submit their applications before the deadline.

Final Thoughts

The UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 present a powerful opportunity for organizations and innovators working at the intersection of sustainability, industrial transformation, and global development.

Whether through climate innovation, ethical supply chains, women’s empowerment, or scalable industrial technologies, the awards celebrate solutions capable of driving long-term positive change for people, industries, and the planet.

For companies seeking global recognition, international partnerships, and meaningful impact, the ONE World Sustainability Awards offer one of the most credible and influential sustainability platforms in the world.

Learn more and apply

Interested applicants can apply through the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards application portal HERE.

Click HERE to learn more about the UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post UNIDO ONE World Sustainability Awards 2026 Applications Open: Global Recognition for Sustainable Innovation and Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: develoPPP Ventures 2026 Funding Programme for Start-ups in Africa | Catalytic Growth Funding for Innovative Businesses in Ghana, Kenya, Rwanda, South Africa, and Tanzania
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
African start-ups are increasingly becoming powerful drivers of innovation, job creation, economic transformation, and sustainable development across the continent. However, one of the biggest challenges facing early-stage and growth-stage businesses remains access to catalytic financing that can help them scale their impact and expand operations sustainably. To address this challenge, the develoPPP Ventures Funding Programme […]

The post develoPPP Ventures 2026 Funding Programme for Start-ups in Africa | Catalytic Growth Funding for Innovative Businesses in Ghana, Kenya, Rwanda, South Africa, and Tanzania appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
African start-ups are increasingly becoming powerful drivers of innovation, job creation, economic transformation, and sustainable development across the continent. However, one of the biggest challenges facing early-stage and growth-stage businesses remains access to catalytic financing that can help them scale their impact and expand operations sustainably.

To address this challenge, the develoPPP Ventures Funding Programme 2026 is offering an exciting opportunity for innovative start-ups operating in selected African countries to access growth financing and strategic support. The programme is designed to support businesses with scalable and development-oriented business models that contribute to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

The current call for applications is open from May 15 to June 30, 2026, and targets start-ups operating in Ghana, Kenya, Rwanda, South Africa, and Tanzania.

Implemented on behalf of the German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ), the programme is supported by implementing partners including DEG Impulse gGmbH and Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH.

Start-ups with innovative and scalable business models that create measurable development impact are strongly encouraged to apply.

 

What is develoPPP Ventures?

develoPPP Ventures is a funding initiative that supports start-ups in developing and emerging markets by providing catalytic funding for growth investments. The programme aims to strengthen entrepreneurial ecosystems and encourage private-sector solutions that address social, environmental, and economic challenges.

The programme specifically targets innovative start-ups that:

  • Already demonstrate proof of concept through initial revenues
  • Have strong potential for scalability
  • Contribute positively to sustainable development
  • Create measurable development impact
  • Operate with innovative business solutions

Unlike traditional grants, develoPPP Ventures focuses on businesses that combine commercial viability with development relevance.

 

Objectives of the Programme

The programme seeks to:

  1. Support innovative start-ups in emerging markets
  2. Encourage sustainable and inclusive economic growth
  3. Promote business models aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
  4. Increase employment and entrepreneurship opportunities
  5. Strengthen local economies through impactful innovation
  6. Enable scalable businesses to attract further investment

By supporting high-potential start-ups, the programme helps businesses move from early growth stages toward long-term sustainability and expansion.

 

Current Target Countries for the 2026 Call

The ongoing develoPPP Ventures application cycle specifically targets start-ups operating in the following African countries:

  • Ghana
  • Kenya
  • Rwanda
  • South Africa
  • Tanzania

The programme provides local support teams to guide applicants during the application and selection process.

 

Funding Benefits for Selected Start-ups

Selected start-ups under the develoPPP Ventures programme may benefit from:

  • Catalytic co-financed growth investment
  • Financial support for scaling operations
  • Increased business visibility and credibility
  • Access to development-focused investment ecosystems
  • Support from international development partners
  • Opportunity to strengthen sustainable business impact
  • Enhanced potential for attracting additional investors and partners

The programme is particularly valuable for start-ups seeking strategic growth support while contributing to sustainable development outcomes.

 

Participation Requirements

To qualify for develoPPP Ventures 2026, applicants must meet the following participation requirements:

1. Company Registration Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Already be registered in the target country
    OR
  • Plan to register in the target country before receiving the co-financed investment

 

2. Innovative Business Model

The start-up must pursue an innovative business model and already generate initial revenues.

This means applicants should demonstrate:

  • Proof of concept
  • Existing customer traction
  • Market validation
  • Revenue-generating activities

 

3. Development Relevance and Scalability

Applicants must show that their business model:

  • Has strong development relevance
  • Is scalable
  • Contributes to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

The programme prioritizes businesses capable of creating broad social or economic impact while maintaining commercial sustainability.

 

What Makes a Business Model Eligible?

The programme considers several important factors when evaluating start-ups.

Development Relevance

A business model is considered development-relevant when it:

  • Creates jobs
  • Improves livelihoods
  • Enhances sustainability
  • Solves social or environmental challenges
  • Supports inclusive economic growth

 

Innovation

The programme seeks businesses with innovative approaches, products, technologies, or services that address existing market challenges differently and effectively.

 

Scalability

Eligible businesses should demonstrate potential for expansion, replication, and long-term growth within or beyond their current markets.

 

Application Period

The current application window for develoPPP Ventures 2026 is:

May 15 to June 30, 2026

Applications submitted after the deadline may not be considered.

 

How to Apply

Eligible start-ups can submit and manage their applications through the official application platforms depending on their country of operation.

For Start-ups in Ghana or South Africa

Applications are handled through the platform managed by Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH.

Application Platform:

GIZ Application Platform

 

For Start-ups in Kenya, Rwanda, or Tanzania

Applications are managed through the platform supported by DEG Impulse gGmbH.

Application Platform:


develoPPP Ventures Official Website

 

Selection Process

Applications are reviewed through a competitive selection process involving local experts and programme partners.

The evaluation process may assess:

  • Business viability
  • Innovation
  • Financial sustainability
  • Development impact
  • Scalability potential
  • Alignment with SDGs
  • Team capacity and leadership

Applicants may also need to provide additional documents during later stages of the evaluation process.

 

Why African Start-ups Should Apply

Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem is rapidly expanding, and programmes like develoPPP Ventures provide critical support for businesses seeking to grow sustainably while addressing local and global development challenges.

This programme is particularly beneficial for start-ups that:

  • Need catalytic funding to scale
  • Already have validated business models
  • Want to expand operations in African markets
  • Aim to create social and economic impact
  • Seek international development partnerships
  • Focus on sustainability and SDG-driven innovation

 

Key Advantages of the Programme

Some of the major advantages of develoPPP Ventures include:

1. Strategic Growth Financing

The programme supports start-ups at a critical growth stage where traditional financing can be difficult to access.

2. International Development Support

Selected businesses gain visibility through partnerships with globally recognized development organizations.

3. Strong Focus on Sustainability

The initiative prioritizes businesses contributing to long-term sustainable development goals.

4. Access to Local Expertise

Applicants receive support from local teams familiar with the regional business environment.

 

Contact Information

General Questions About the Programme

For general inquiries regarding develoPPP and BMZ funding programmes:

  • Email: beratung@wirtschaft-entwicklung.de

 

Country-Specific Investment Questions

Ghana and South Africa

  • Email: develoPPPVentures@giz.de

Kenya, Rwanda, and Tanzania

  • Email: develoPPPVentures@degimpulse.de

 

Official Programme Website

Official Website:


develoPPP Ventures Official Programme Page

 

Final Thoughts

The develoPPP Ventures Funding Programme 2026 represents a major opportunity for African start-ups seeking catalytic financing and strategic support to scale impactful businesses. With a strong emphasis on innovation, sustainability, and development impact, the programme provides an important platform for entrepreneurs building solutions that address real-world challenges across Africa.

If your start-up already has proof of concept, generates initial revenues, and aims to create measurable development impact, this could be the ideal opportunity to secure growth support and strengthen your expansion strategy.

 

  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post develoPPP Ventures 2026 Funding Programme for Start-ups in Africa | Catalytic Growth Funding for Innovative Businesses in Ghana, Kenya, Rwanda, South Africa, and Tanzania appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest 2026 in the USA | Win a $1,000 Tuition Award for Law School
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Pursuing a legal career is a dream for many students who want to make a meaningful impact in society through justice, advocacy, and public service. However, the cost of legal education in the United States can be financially overwhelming for many aspiring lawyers. To support future legal professionals, Andrew Flusche Attorney at Law is offering […]

The post Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest 2026 in the USA | Win a $1,000 Tuition Award for Law School appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Pursuing a legal career is a dream for many students who want to make a meaningful impact in society through justice, advocacy, and public service. However, the cost of legal education in the United States can be financially overwhelming for many aspiring lawyers. To support future legal professionals, Andrew Flusche Attorney at Law is offering the Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest 2026, a scholarship opportunity designed to assist law students with tuition expenses while encouraging them to reflect on their passion for the legal profession.

The scholarship contest provides a $1,000 tuition award to one selected winner enrolled in an accredited law school in the United States. Applicants are invited to submit a compelling essay explaining their inspiration for becoming a lawyer, their future career goals, and how they plan to make a difference in their communities through the legal profession.

This scholarship not only provides financial support but also gives students an opportunity to express their vision for justice, leadership, and service in society.

 

About the Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest

The scholarship is sponsored by Andrew Flusche, an attorney who pursued law because of his passion for helping people directly through legal representation and advocacy.

According to the scholarship announcement, the contest seeks students who are motivated to enter the legal profession for meaningful reasons and who demonstrate a strong commitment to making a positive impact throughout their careers.

The scholarship specifically asks applicants to explain:

  • What inspired them to pursue a legal career
  • Their long-term professional goals
  • Why they deserve to receive the scholarship award
  • How they hope to contribute positively to their communities through law

 

Scholarship Award Value

One (1) winner will receive:

  • A $1,000 scholarship award
  • Financial support toward law school tuition expenses
  • Recognition as the selected scholarship recipient

Although the award amount may not cover the entirety of tuition fees, it can significantly help students with educational expenses, books, legal materials, or other academic costs associated with law school.

 

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest 2026, applicants must meet the following requirement:

  • Be enrolled at an accredited law school located in the United States

Students planning to attend law school may also apply if they can provide proof of enrollment at the institution.

 

Essay Prompt for Applicants

Applicants are required to submit a compelling essay addressing the following topics:

  1. What inspired you to pursue a legal career?
  2. What goals do you hope to achieve throughout your legal career?
  3. Why should you be considered for the scholarship award?

The essay must demonstrate originality, clarity, ambition, and a genuine passion for the legal profession.

 

Essay Requirements

Applicants must ensure that their essay follows these specifications:

  • Minimum of 250 words
  • Maximum of 350 words
  • Must directly address the scholarship prompt
  • Should be clear, compelling, and professionally written

Strong essays are likely to highlight:

  • Personal experiences
  • Leadership qualities
  • Commitment to justice
  • Community service aspirations
  • Long-term professional impact

 

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following materials:

  • First and last name
  • Phone number
  • Email address
  • Mailing address
  • Name of the law school attended or planned
  • Proof of enrollment
  • Letter of recommendation
  • Scholarship essay

All documents must be combined into one single PDF file in the following order:

  1. Proof of enrollment
  2. Letter of recommendation
  3. Essay

Failure to follow the required order or file format may affect the application.

 

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest 2026 is:

July 30, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid technical issues or last-minute errors.

 

Winner Announcement Date

The selected winner will be contacted through the email address provided in the application on:

August 30, 2026

Only one winner will be selected for the scholarship contest.

 

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the official scholarship application form available on the official website of Andrew Flusche Attorney at Law Scholarship Page.

Applicants should carefully review all submission requirements before uploading their PDF file.

 

Tips for Writing a Strong Scholarship Essay

To improve your chances of winning the scholarship, consider the following tips:

1. Share Your Personal Story

Explain the experiences that inspired your interest in law and legal advocacy.

2. Demonstrate Passion

Clearly show why the legal profession matters to you and how committed you are to making a difference.

3. Highlight Career Goals

Discuss your future plans, including areas of law you wish to practice and the impact you hope to create.

4. Be Authentic

Avoid generic statements. Use real-life examples and personal reflections to make your essay memorable.

5. Proofread Carefully

Ensure your essay is free from grammar, spelling, and formatting errors before submission.

 

Why You Should Apply

Law school can be financially demanding, and scholarships like this provide meaningful assistance to students pursuing legal education. Beyond the financial benefit, the scholarship also gives students a platform to articulate their values, ambitions, and dedication to justice.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for students who:

  • Have a strong passion for advocacy and justice
  • Want to contribute positively to their communities
  • Need additional financial support for legal studies
  • Aspire to become impactful legal professionals

 

Official Scholarship Contact Information

For questions regarding the scholarship contest, applicants may contact:

  • Email: reception@andrewflusche.com
  • Phone: 540-318-5824

Official Website:
Andrew Flusche Attorney at Law Official Website

 

Final Thoughts

The Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest 2026 offers aspiring lawyers an excellent opportunity to gain financial support while sharing their passion for the legal profession. With a straightforward application process and a meaningful essay prompt, students have the chance to showcase their ambitions, leadership potential, and commitment to serving society through law.

If you are currently enrolled in a U.S. law school and have a strong vision for your future legal career, this scholarship could help support your educational journey and professional aspirations.

Apply here

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Scholarship  opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Andrew Flusche Law School Scholarship Contest 2026 in the USA | Win a $1,000 Tuition Award for Law School appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026: Win a Share of $300,000 to Scale Your Impact and Fight Extreme Poverty Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026, a globally recognized opportunity supporting changemakers, social entrepreneurs, innovators, nonprofit leaders, and impact-driven individuals working to end extreme poverty around the world. Celebrating its twelfth year, the awards continue to empower visionary leaders who are creating measurable social impact through innovative and scalable […]

The post Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026: Win a Share of $300,000 to Scale Your Impact and Fight Extreme Poverty Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the prestigious Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026, a globally recognized opportunity supporting changemakers, social entrepreneurs, innovators, nonprofit leaders, and impact-driven individuals working to end extreme poverty around the world. Celebrating its twelfth year, the awards continue to empower visionary leaders who are creating measurable social impact through innovative and scalable solutions.

Organized by Global Citizen in partnership with the Waislitz Foundation, the annual awards offer three major cash prizes totaling $300,000 USD. Beyond financial support, winners receive global recognition, editorial features, social media exposure, and access to an international network of social impact leaders and advocates.

If you are leading a transformative initiative addressing poverty, inequality, education, healthcare, food systems, climate resilience, or community empowerment, this could be one of the most important global awards to apply for in 2026.

Applications are open until June 14, 2026.

About the Global Citizen Waislitz Awards

The Global Citizen Waislitz Awards were established to recognize individuals who are taking bold action to improve lives and accelerate the end of extreme poverty.

The awards are funded by the Waislitz Foundation, founded by Australian philanthropist Alex Waislitz. The foundation supports innovative initiatives and social impact projects that empower communities and create measurable global change.

Over the years, the awards have supported entrepreneurs, innovators, nonprofit founders, climate leaders, education advocates, healthcare innovators, and grassroots activists from different regions of the world.

The awards not only provide financial support but also amplify the visibility of impactful projects and help winners expand their reach internationally.

According to Global Citizen, the awards celebrate people who demonstrate innovation, scalability, adaptability, and measurable social impact in their efforts to tackle poverty and inequality.

Total Prize Value

The 2026 edition features three awards totaling $300,000 USD:

1. Global Citizen Waislitz Prize – $100,000

This is the grand prize awarded to an individual who demonstrates excellence across all judging criteria, including innovation, impact, scalability, adaptability, and global citizenship.

2. Disruptor Award – $100,000

This prize recognizes an innovator or changemaker whose work significantly disrupts systems that contribute to extreme poverty through creative and measurable solutions.

3. Global Citizens’ Choice Award – $100,000

This award is selected with participation from the Global Citizen community through online voting.

All winners also receive extensive media visibility through Global Citizen’s editorial and social media platforms.

Why the Global Citizen Waislitz Awards Matter

Extreme poverty continues to affect millions of people worldwide. However, across communities and countries, there are individuals developing practical and innovative solutions to tackle these challenges.

The Global Citizen Waislitz Awards provide:

  1. Financial support to scale social impact initiatives
  2. International recognition for changemakers
  3. Access to global audiences and supporters
  4. Opportunities for collaboration and partnerships
  5. A platform to amplify advocacy and impact stories
  6. Increased credibility for social enterprises and nonprofits
  7. Exposure to investors, donors, and development organizations

For many past winners, the award became a catalyst for growth, expansion, and increased global influence.

Evaluation Criteria

Applicants are evaluated based on five major criteria:

1. Global Citizenship

Applicants should demonstrate commitment to the values and practices of global citizenship through their work and engagement.

2. Proof of Concept

Candidates must show measurable impact and evidence that their solution works effectively. Applicants are expected to have at least one to two years of demonstrated impact addressing extreme poverty.

3. Disruption

Judges will assess whether the initiative disrupts traditional systems and introduces innovative solutions that challenge the root causes of poverty.

4. Scalability

Applicants should explain how the award funding would help scale or strengthen their work and reach more communities.

5. Adaptability

Candidates must demonstrate resilience and the ability to adapt to changing environments and emerging challenges.

Who Should Apply?

The Global Citizen Waislitz Awards are ideal for:

  • Social entrepreneurs
  • Nonprofit founders
  • Community leaders
  • Youth changemakers
  • Healthcare innovators
  • Education advocates
  • Climate action leaders
  • Human rights activists
  • Women-led impact initiatives
  • Poverty reduction innovators
  • Grassroots organizations
  • Sustainable development practitioners
  • Food security innovators
  • Renewable energy entrepreneurs
  • Economic empowerment leaders

The awards welcome applications from individuals creating measurable social impact in their communities and beyond.

What Makes a Strong Application?

Successful applications typically demonstrate:

  • Clear measurable impact
  • Strong leadership and vision
  • Innovation and creativity
  • Sustainable solutions
  • Community engagement
  • Evidence-based outcomes
  • Scalability potential
  • Commitment to ending poverty

Applicants should clearly explain:

  • The problem they are solving
  • Their solution and approach
  • The communities impacted
  • Results achieved so far
  • Future plans for scaling
  • How the funding would strengthen their work

Previous Winners and Their Impact

The Global Citizen Waislitz Awards have supported remarkable changemakers worldwide.

Osei Boateng – Ghana

Founder of the OKB Hope Foundation, he delivers mobile healthcare services to underserved communities and has supported thousands with preventive healthcare and mental health services.

Dysmus Kisilu – Kenya

Founder of Solar Freeze, which provides solar-powered cold storage for farmers to reduce food loss and improve agricultural productivity.

Peter Njeri – Kenya

Co-founder of Mega Gas Alternative Energy, converting plastic waste into clean cooking gas solutions for low-income households.

Kristin Kagetsu – India

Co-founder of Saathi, a social enterprise addressing menstrual health access through sustainable products.

Bina Shrestha – Nepal

Co-founder of Build up Nepal, which promotes affordable and sustainable housing solutions after the Nepal earthquake.

These examples demonstrate the wide range of social impact areas supported through the awards.

Benefits of Applying

Applicants and winners can gain numerous advantages, including:

  • Up to $100,000 in funding
  • International media exposure
  • Global recognition
  • Networking opportunities
  • Increased credibility
  • Partnership opportunities
  • Greater visibility for social impact initiatives
  • Access to the Global Citizen platform
  • Community support and engagement
  • Opportunity to scale impact globally

Application Deadline

Applications for the Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026 are open until:

June 14, 2026

Applicants are encouraged to submit early and prepare strong evidence-based applications.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the Official Global Citizen Waislitz Awards Platform.

Tips for a Successful Application

To improve your chances:

  1. Clearly quantify your impact
  2. Use data and evidence
  3. Highlight innovation and uniqueness
  4. Demonstrate sustainability
  5. Explain long-term scalability
  6. Share authentic stories and testimonials
  7. Emphasize measurable outcomes
  8. Show how funding will accelerate impact

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Are organizations eligible to apply?

Yes. The awards recognize impactful initiatives and social impact work led by individuals and organizations.

Are nonprofit organizations the only eligible applicants?

No. Different forms of social impact initiatives may be considered, provided they demonstrate measurable impact.

Can early-stage innovators apply?

Applicants are expected to demonstrate at least one to two years of measurable impact.

Is English required for the application?

The official FAQ section should be consulted for language requirements and application guidelines.

How are winners selected?

Winners are selected through evaluation against the established judging criteria, while the Global Citizens’ Choice Award includes public voting input.

Click HERE to learn more about the Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026.

Final Thoughts

The Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026 represent more than just financial support. They are an opportunity for visionary changemakers to gain global recognition, scale their impact, and inspire communities worldwide.

As inequality, climate challenges, food insecurity, and poverty continue to affect millions globally, innovative leaders remain essential in creating sustainable solutions. This award provides both the resources and the international platform needed to accelerate transformative impact.

If you are building solutions that improve lives and challenge systems that perpetuate poverty, this is your opportunity to amplify your mission globally.

Discover more global Award opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Global Citizen Waislitz Awards 2026: Win a Share of $300,000 to Scale Your Impact and Fight Extreme Poverty Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: University of New South Wales Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugee Students 2026 in Australia: Full Tuition, Accommodation, and Living Support Available
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The UNSW Sydney has officially opened applications for the UNSW Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugee Students Commencing Term 3, 2026. These life-changing scholarships are designed to support students from refugee and asylum seeker backgrounds by providing access to higher education opportunities at one of Australia’s leading universities. The scholarship program offers a combination of: […]

The post University of New South Wales Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugee Students 2026 in Australia: Full Tuition, Accommodation, and Living Support Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNSW Sydney has officially opened applications for the UNSW Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugee Students Commencing Term 3, 2026. These life-changing scholarships are designed to support students from refugee and asylum seeker backgrounds by providing access to higher education opportunities at one of Australia’s leading universities.

The scholarship program offers a combination of:

  • Full tuition fee support
  • Accommodation assistance
  • Annual living stipends
  • Financial support for study-related costs

UNSW aims to remove financial barriers and provide displaced students with the opportunity to pursue undergraduate and postgraduate coursework studies in a supportive academic environment.

Applications for the scholarships close on 18 June 2026.

About UNSW Sydney

UNSW Sydney is one of Australia’s top-ranked universities and a member of the prestigious Group of Eight (Go8), a coalition of leading Australian research-intensive universities.

Located in Sydney, UNSW is globally recognized for:

  • Academic excellence
  • Research innovation
  • Social impact initiatives
  • International student support
  • Inclusive education opportunities

The university is strongly committed to:

  • Equity in education
  • Supporting marginalized communities
  • Expanding access to higher education
  • Creating pathways for displaced and vulnerable students

Through these scholarships, UNSW seeks to empower asylum seekers and refugee students to achieve academic success and long-term career opportunities.

Overview of the UNSW Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugee Students 2026

The scholarship program supports eligible students commencing their first term of studies at UNSW in Term 3, 2026.

The scholarships available under this application process include:

  1. Sanctuary Scholarship for People Seeking Asylum and Refugees with Temporary Protection
  2. HMJ Sassafras Scholarship

Applicants only need to submit one application under the UNSW Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugees Commencing Term 3, 2026 (UGCE1420) to be considered for multiple scholarship opportunities.

More scholarship opportunities may also be added progressively.

Scholarship Benefits

The scholarships provide extensive financial support designed to help students focus on their studies without the burden of major financial stress.

1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage

Selected students may receive:

  • Full tuition fee scholarships for the minimum duration of their degree program

This support applies to eligible undergraduate and postgraduate coursework programs.

2. Accommodation Support

Successful applicants may receive:

  • On-campus UNSW accommodation support worth up to AUD $25,000 per year

This benefit significantly reduces living expenses for students relocating for study.

3. Annual Living Stipend

Students may also receive:

  • AUD $10,000 annual stipend
  • AUD $5,000 annual stipend (depending on scholarship category)

The stipend can help cover:

  • Daily living expenses
  • Study materials
  • Transportation
  • Food and personal expenses

Scholarship Options Available

Sanctuary Scholarship for People Seeking Asylum and Refugees with Temporary Protection (UGCE1411)

This scholarship includes:

  • Full tuition fee scholarship
  • Accommodation support up to AUD $25,000 annually
  • AUD $10,000 or AUD $5,000 annual stipend

Eligible applicants must:

  • Commence an eligible undergraduate or postgraduate coursework degree in Term 3, 2026
  • Hold an eligible humanitarian or protection visa

HMJ Sassafras Scholarship (UGCE1534)

This scholarship provides:

  • Full tuition fee scholarship
  • Accommodation support up to AUD $25,000 annually
  • AUD $10,000 annual stipend

This scholarship is specifically available to students commencing eligible undergraduate coursework programs.

Eligible Visa Categories

Applicants must hold one of the following visa types:

  • Temporary Protection Visa (Subclass 785)
  • Safe Haven Enterprise Visa (Subclass 790)
  • Bridging Visa
  • Temporary Humanitarian Concern Visa (Subclass 786)
  • Humanitarian Stay (Temporary) Visa (Subclass 449)

Important conditions include:

  • The visa must be valid by the application deadline
  • Bridging visa holders must provide documentation from a recognized organization
  • Applicants must have Australian study rights

Additional Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the scholarships, applicants must:

1. Be an Asylum Seeker or Refugee

Applicants must hold an eligible humanitarian or refugee-related visa.

2. Receive an Offer of Admission

Applicants must:

  • Receive an offer of admission into an eligible coursework program at UNSW for Term 3, 2026 by 18 June 2026

Students awaiting admission decisions may still apply for the scholarship. However, scholarship offers will only be finalized after an unconditional admission offer has been issued.

Required Identification Documents

Applicants must provide one of the following:

  • Passport
  • ImmiCard
  • Titre de Voyage
  • PLO56 (M56)
  • Document for Travel to Australia (DFTTA)

Visa and documentation status will be verified using the Visa Entitlement Verification Online (VEVO) service.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on several important factors.

Academic Merit

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong academic performance
  • Motivation for higher education
  • Potential for academic success at UNSW

Commitment to Education

UNSW also evaluates:

  • Dedication to studies
  • Educational aspirations
  • Capacity to contribute positively to the university community

Personal Statement Requirement

Applicants must submit a personal statement of no more than 500 words.

The statement should explain:

  • Individual background and circumstances
  • Educational challenges faced
  • Obstacles encountered as an asylum seeker or refugee
  • Motivation for pursuing higher education
  • Future goals and aspirations

UNSW emphasizes that unsupported claims or incomplete responses will not be assessed.

Important Application Dates

Applications Open:

1 April 2026

Application Deadline:

18 June 2026 at 11:59 PM AEST

Late applications and incomplete supporting documents will not be accepted.

How to Apply

Interested students should follow these steps:

  1. Register an account on the UNSW Scholarship Application Online portal
  2. Complete the UGCE1420 scholarship application
  3. Upload required documents
  4. Submit a personal statement
  5. Ensure admission application to UNSW has been completed
  6. Submit the application before the deadline

Applicants can apply to UNSW coursework programs:

  • Through UAC
  • Directly via apply.unsw.edu.au

Apply, Click Here

Why This Scholarship Matters

Access to higher education remains one of the greatest challenges facing displaced students and refugee communities worldwide.

The UNSW Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugee Students demonstrate a strong commitment to:

  • Educational inclusion
  • Humanitarian support
  • Equity and diversity
  • Empowerment through education

By covering tuition, accommodation, and living costs, these scholarships provide students with the stability and support needed to focus on academic achievement and future career development.

Why Students Should Apply

This scholarship opportunity offers much more than financial support. Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • Studying at a globally respected university
  • Access to high-quality education
  • A supportive and inclusive campus environment
  • Opportunities for professional and personal growth
  • Long-term career opportunities after graduation

For students rebuilding their educational pathways after displacement or hardship, this scholarship can be a transformative opportunity.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover global scholarship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post University of New South Wales Scholarships for Asylum Seekers and Refugee Students 2026 in Australia: Full Tuition, Accommodation, and Living Support Available appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SAIL Scholarship 2026: $7,500 Funding Opportunity for Outstanding Future Law Leaders
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The NAPABA Law Foundation has officially opened applications for the highly competitive SAIL Scholarship 2026, a prestigious funding opportunity designed to support promising law students who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, public service commitment, and dedication to Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) communities. This scholarship program is specifically targeted at current first-year law students […]

The post SAIL Scholarship 2026: $7,500 Funding Opportunity for Outstanding Future Law Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The NAPABA Law Foundation has officially opened applications for the highly competitive SAIL Scholarship 2026, a prestigious funding opportunity designed to support promising law students who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, public service commitment, and dedication to Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) communities.

This scholarship program is specifically targeted at current first-year law students (1Ls) who will advance into their second year of law school (2L) during the Fall 2026 semester. The scholarship aims to empower emerging legal professionals who are passionate about making meaningful contributions within their communities and the legal profession.

The SAIL Scholarship is made possible through the generosity of Sharon and Ivan Fong, whose support continues to help shape the next generation of impactful legal leaders.

What is the SAIL Scholarship?

The SAIL Scholarship is an academic and leadership-based scholarship initiative that recognizes law students with exceptional promise in the legal field. Beyond financial support, the program seeks applicants who embody leadership, service, cultural awareness, and a long-term commitment to giving back to their communities.

The scholarship particularly values students who have shown a deep understanding of social and cultural issues affecting one or more AAPI communities or who are committed to creating positive change in those communities.

This opportunity is ideal for ambitious law students who aspire to become influential advocates, policymakers, public interest attorneys, community leaders, or legal professionals dedicated to equity and justice.

Scholarship Benefits

Successful applicants for the SAIL Scholarship will receive:

  • Total Award Value: $7,500
  • The scholarship funding will be distributed equally over two years
  • First disbursement will be made in August during the recipient’s second year of law school
  • Second disbursement will be made in August during the recipient’s third year of law school

The continuation of the second disbursement depends on:

  1. Maintaining strong academic performance
  2. Demonstrating good character
  3. Continuing dedication to the scholarship’s core values and mission

This structured funding model encourages long-term academic consistency and leadership development throughout law school.

Who Should Apply?

The SAIL Scholarship is designed for highly motivated law students who demonstrate excellence both inside and outside the classroom.

Applicants are expected to show evidence of:

1. Academic Excellence

Candidates should have a strong academic background from:

  • Undergraduate studies
  • Their first year of law school

Academic achievement is a major evaluation factor for the scholarship committee.

2. Leadership Potential

Applicants should possess strong leadership qualities demonstrated through:

  • Student organizations
  • Community initiatives
  • Campus leadership
  • Advocacy programs
  • Professional associations
  • Mentorship roles

The foundation is looking for future legal leaders capable of driving positive change.

3. Volunteerism and Public Interest Service

Students with a history of service and community engagement are strongly encouraged to apply.

Examples may include:

  • Legal aid initiatives
  • Community outreach
  • Public service projects
  • Advocacy work
  • Volunteer organizations
  • Social justice efforts

4. Commitment to AAPI Communities

The scholarship places significant value on applicants who:

  • Understand social and cultural issues impacting AAPI communities
  • Have contributed to AAPI advocacy
  • Plan to make a long-term impact on AAPI-related legal or social issues

Applicants do not necessarily need to focus exclusively on AAPI issues, but they should demonstrate meaningful awareness or commitment.

5. “Pay It Forward” Mentality

One of the scholarship’s core principles is community impact.

Applicants should demonstrate a willingness to:

  • Support future generations
  • Mentor others
  • Contribute to the legal profession
  • Strengthen their communities through service and leadership

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the SAIL Scholarship 2026, applicants must:

  • Be a current 1L law student
  • Be entering 2L in Fall 2026
  • Demonstrate strong academic and leadership potential
  • Show commitment to service and community impact
  • Meet the scholarship’s evaluation criteria

Interested applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official FAQ section before submitting their application to fully understand all expectations and requirements.

Why This Scholarship Matters

Law school can be financially demanding, and scholarships like SAIL provide critical support that allows students to focus on academic success and leadership growth.

More importantly, the scholarship represents an investment in future legal professionals who are committed to advocacy, inclusion, and meaningful community impact.

Programs like this help cultivate a stronger and more diverse legal profession while empowering students to pursue careers in public interest, corporate law, policy, civil rights, and beyond.

Application Deadline

Deadline: June 30, 2026, at 5:00 PM ET

Applicants are strongly advised to begin their applications early to avoid last-minute technical issues and to ensure all required materials are submitted before the deadline.

How to Apply

Click here to apply

For more information about this scholarship, Click here

Fore more global scholarships Click here

The post SAIL Scholarship 2026: $7,500 Funding Opportunity for Outstanding Future Law Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Resilience Fellowship 2026 for East African Youth: Fully Supported 9-Month Leadership and Mental Health Fellowship for Climate Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Young climate leaders across East Africa now have an exciting opportunity to join the transformative Resilience Fellowship 2026, a youth-centered leadership and wellbeing program designed to equip changemakers with the tools, networks, emotional resilience, and leadership skills needed to navigate climate activism and community work sustainably. Organized by The Resilience Project, the fellowship is returning […]

The post Resilience Fellowship 2026 for East African Youth: Fully Supported 9-Month Leadership and Mental Health Fellowship for Climate Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young climate leaders across East Africa now have an exciting opportunity to join the transformative Resilience Fellowship 2026, a youth-centered leadership and wellbeing program designed to equip changemakers with the tools, networks, emotional resilience, and leadership skills needed to navigate climate activism and community work sustainably.

Organized by The Resilience Project, the fellowship is returning for its fifth edition and is currently recruiting passionate young leaders from Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, and Tanzania.

The 2026 cohort will select 12 pairs of East African changemakers aged 18–28 to participate in a highly immersive 9-month fellowship journey focused on healing, resilience, climate justice, mental wellbeing, mentorship, and grassroots community leadership.

Unlike many traditional fellowships, the Resilience Fellowship is intentionally designed by youth for youth, creating a deeply human-centered experience that prioritizes emotional wellbeing, collaboration, and sustainable activism.

About the Resilience Fellowship 2026

Climate activism and community leadership can be deeply rewarding, but they can also lead to emotional exhaustion, burnout, climate anxiety, and isolation. The Resilience Fellowship was created to address these realities by helping young changemakers build healthier, stronger, and more resilient ways of leading change.

The fellowship provides participants with a safe and empowering space to:

  • Deepen their activism
  • Improve emotional wellbeing
  • Build supportive peer relationships
  • Strengthen community resilience
  • Learn facilitation and leadership skills
  • Receive mentorship from experienced leaders
  • Explore healing and restorative practices

The program combines personal development, peer-led facilitation, community engagement, mentorship, and mental health training into one comprehensive fellowship experience.

What Fellows Will Receive

Selected fellows will gain access to a wide range of professional, personal, and leadership benefits throughout the fellowship period.

1. Fully Supported 5-Day Residential Retreat

Participants will attend a 5-day in-person Resilience Residential designed to promote restoration, healing, reflection, and connection in nature.

The retreat includes:

  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Meaningful peer discussions
  • Wellness and healing sessions
  • Restorative activities
  • Community-building experiences

This immersive experience is one of the fellowship’s core highlights and helps fellows reconnect with themselves and others in a supportive environment.

2. Mental Health First Aid Certification

Fellows will receive professional training and certification as Mental Health First Aiders, equipping them with practical skills to support emotional wellbeing within their communities.

This certification adds both personal and professional value while strengthening community mental health support systems.

3. Leadership and Facilitation Skills

Participants will gain practical experience and training in:

  • Facilitation
  • Community leadership
  • Project management
  • Emotional support practices
  • Community care strategies
  • Peer engagement techniques

These skills are designed to help fellows lead more effectively within climate and social justice spaces.

4. Monthly Mentorship

Over the course of 8 months, fellows will receive guidance from experienced intergenerational mentors who will support their personal growth, leadership journey, and community initiatives.

Mentorship sessions will help participants:

  • Navigate challenges
  • Strengthen leadership confidence
  • Improve project implementation
  • Build long-term resilience

5. Community Support and Lifelong Networks

The fellowship strongly emphasizes connection and solidarity.

Participants will become part of a powerful regional network of young East African changemakers committed to climate justice, emotional wellbeing, and sustainable activism.

Many fellows develop long-lasting friendships, collaborations, and professional partnerships during the program.

6. Financial Support

Selected fellows will receive:

  • A KES 40,000 stipend
  • Fully covered travel costs

This financial support helps reduce participation barriers and ensures fellows can fully engage in the program.

Fellowship Timeline

The Resilience Fellowship runs from:

July 2026 to March 2027

During this period, fellows will participate in training sessions, mentorship, facilitation activities, community engagement, and reporting responsibilities.

Fellowship Commitments and Responsibilities

The fellowship is described as a high-commitment program, and applicants are expected to fully participate in all activities throughout the 9-month journey.

Fellows Must Commit To:

1. Mental Health First Aid Training

Participants must complete:

  • A 2-day online Mental Health First Aid training program

2. Attendance at the Residential Retreat

Selected fellows must fully attend the in-person residential event scheduled for:

31 July – 4 August 2026

3. Co-Leading Resilience Circles

Fellows will facilitate community-based Resilience Circles involving:

  • 16–20 young people
  • Weekly sessions over 8 weeks
  • Approximately 5–6 hours per week for preparation and facilitation

These circles are designed to strengthen emotional resilience and community wellbeing among young people.

4. Weekly Reporting and Coordination

Participants will be responsible for:

  • Submitting feedback after sessions
  • Reporting progress and data
  • Connecting with the TRP team weekly

5. Monthly Check-Ins

Fellows must join monthly community calls for:

  • Updates
  • Reflection
  • Problem-solving
  • Peer learning

6. Monthly Mentorship Meetings

Each fellow will meet with their assigned mentor for one hour every month.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is seeking passionate young changemakers who are committed to climate justice, emotional wellbeing, and community leadership.

Ideal applicants are individuals who:

  • Care deeply about social and environmental issues
  • Want to strengthen their leadership capacity
  • Are open to emotional growth and healing
  • Value collaboration and community care
  • Are interested in mental health and resilience-building

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Resilience Fellowship 2026, applicants must:

  • Be between 18–28 years old
  • Be based in Kenya, Uganda, Rwanda, or Tanzania
  • Be available from July 2026 to March 2027
  • Apply with a partner
  • Be passionate about climate justice and resilient communities
  • Be proficient in English
  • Be willing to facilitate community Resilience Circles
  • Be open to conversations around climate anxiety, burnout, and emotional wellbeing

Important Requirement: Applications Must Be Submitted in Pairs

One unique feature of the fellowship is that applicants must apply as pre-formed pairs.

This means:

  • You and your partner should already work well together
  • Both applicants will jointly facilitate Resilience Circles in their local communities
  • The fellowship prioritizes collaboration and peer leadership

Applicants are encouraged to identify partners with complementary strengths and shared values before applying.

Commitment to Inclusion and Diversity

The The Resilience Project strongly emphasizes inclusion and intersectional climate justice.

The fellowship actively welcomes applications from marginalized and underrepresented communities, including young people with lived experiences connected to:

  • Race and ethnicity
  • Gender identity
  • Sexual orientation
  • Disability
  • Socio-economic inequality
  • Faith and religion

The program values diversity, complexity, and inclusive leadership.

Application Process

Interested applicants must first download the official application booklet, which contains:

  • Detailed application instructions
  • Application guidance
  • Required questions
  • Submission process details

Applicants are expected to prepare all responses before submitting the online application form.

Application Deadline

Applications close at 11:59 PM EAT on Sunday, 31 May 2026.

Click here for more and to apply.

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and carefully prepare thoughtful responses that demonstrate their passion, partnership strength, leadership potential, and commitment to resilient community building.

Why the Resilience Fellowship Matters

Climate activism often focuses heavily on action while overlooking emotional wellbeing and sustainability. The Resilience Fellowship addresses this gap by helping young leaders build emotional resilience alongside practical leadership skills.

This fellowship recognizes that sustainable social change requires healthy, supported, and emotionally grounded leaders.

By combining mentorship, healing, mental health support, leadership development, and grassroots action, the fellowship creates a powerful platform for East African youth to thrive personally while strengthening their communities.

For more global fellowship opportunities Click here.

The post Resilience Fellowship 2026 for East African Youth: Fully Supported 9-Month Leadership and Mental Health Fellowship for Climate Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Impact Project Support Internship 2026: Fully Detailed Guide to a Humanitarian and Fundraising Career Opportunity in Washington, DC
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
​If you are passionate about humanitarian causes, philanthropy, nonprofit development, fundraising, and corporate social responsibility, the Global Impact Project Support Internship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity to gain hands-on experience within an internationally focused organization working to mobilize resources for urgent global challenges. Global Impact is currently recruiting a Project Support Intern to join its […]

The post Global Impact Project Support Internship 2026: Fully Detailed Guide to a Humanitarian and Fundraising Career Opportunity in Washington, DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
​If you are passionate about humanitarian causes, philanthropy, nonprofit development, fundraising, and corporate social responsibility, the Global Impact Project Support Internship 2026 offers an outstanding opportunity to gain hands-on experience within an internationally focused organization working to mobilize resources for urgent global challenges.

Global Impact is currently recruiting a Project Support Intern to join its Partner Solutions Team. This internship is ideal for students and recent graduates interested in nonprofit management, fundraising strategy, corporate social responsibility (CSR), humanitarian response, and international development.

The internship provides direct exposure to nonprofit organizations, foundations, corporations, and philanthropic initiatives while helping interns build practical professional experience in research, communications, client support, and project coordination.

About Global Impact

Global Impact is part of Global Impact Ventures and works to mobilize philanthropic resources to support urgent humanitarian priorities and charitable causes worldwide.

The organization collaborates with:

  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Foundations
  • Corporate partners
  • Humanitarian initiatives
  • Development-focused institutions

Global Impact helps organizations strengthen fundraising strategies, donor engagement, charitable campaigns, and social impact programs.

Through this internship, participants will gain insight into how philanthropic partnerships and fundraising operations function within the nonprofit and corporate sectors.

About the Project Support Internship

The Project Support Intern will work closely with the Partner Solutions Team to support research, business development, fundraising efforts, client coordination, and internal operations.

This role is especially valuable for candidates interested in:

  • International development
  • Humanitarian causes
  • Corporate social responsibility
  • Fundraising strategy
  • Nonprofit operations
  • Client engagement
  • Research and communications

Interns will contribute to projects supporting local, national, and global nonprofit initiatives while learning directly from experienced professionals in the philanthropy sector.

Internship Structure and Duration

Work Schedule

  • 20–36 hours per week

Internship Duration

  • 4 months
  • Possibility of extension beyond four months

Work Arrangement

  • Hybrid internship
  • Interns are expected to work from the Washington, DC headquarters one day per week, typically Wednesdays

Internship Stipend

The internship offers a competitive stipend ranging between:

  • $6,480 – $11,664

The exact amount depends on the intern’s availability and weekly schedule.

Important Location and Visa Information

Global Impact is specifically seeking candidates who are local and able to attend in-person office sessions in Washington, DC.

Important Notice

  • Visa sponsorship is NOT available for this internship

Applicants should therefore already possess legal authorization to work in the United States.

Key Responsibilities of the Internship

The internship combines research, fundraising support, communications, and operational coordination.

Client Deliverables and Research Support

Interns will:

  1. Conduct prospect research to identify fundraising leads
  2. Support outreach strategy development
  3. Compile benchmarking reports for nonprofit and corporate clients
  4. Analyze publicly available research and databases
  5. Draft workshop materials, including:
    • Presentations
    • Agendas
    • Activities
  6. Draft surveys and client communications
  7. Edit and proofread client-facing materials

Business Development Support

Interns will assist with:

  1. Researching trends in:
    • Corporate social responsibility
    • Philanthropy
    • Foundations
    • Nonprofit development
  2. Monitoring relevant industry news and events
  3. Supporting outreach and strategic growth initiatives

Internal Team and Operational Support

Interns may also:

  1. Conduct background research for internal projects
  2. Support team capability-building projects
  3. Maintain client management databases
  4. Organize internal records and files
  5. Assist with operational coordination

Unique Learning Opportunities

One of the most valuable aspects of this internship is the broad exposure interns receive to charitable causes and international philanthropy.

Interns will:

  • Engage with nonprofits and foundations
  • Participate in integrated team meetings
  • Observe client interactions
  • Support strategic planning activities
  • Present a capstone project to executive leadership

This provides practical professional experience rarely available in entry-level internships.

Supervision and Professional Mentorship

The intern will:

  • Report directly to a member of the Partner Solutions Team
  • Receive daily guidance and project oversight
  • Participate in collaborative team environments
  • Present their internship work as a final capstone project

This mentorship structure supports both learning and professional development.

Qualifications and Eligibility Requirements

Global Impact is looking for candidates who are:

  • Recent undergraduate graduates
  • Current bachelor’s students
  • Master’s degree students

Preferred Areas of Interest

Ideal candidates may have interest in:

  • International development
  • Humanitarian response
  • Human rights
  • Economic development
  • Corporate social responsibility
  • Fundraising and philanthropy

Preferred Skills and Experience

Candidates with the following qualifications may be particularly competitive:

Academic or Professional Experience In:

  • Research
  • Writing
  • Client management
  • Fundraising
  • Communications

Essential Skills

  • Excellent writing ability
  • Strong presentation skills
  • Organizational skills
  • Attention to detail
  • Time management
  • Ability to manage multiple deadlines
  • Independent and collaborative work ability

Technical Skills

Strong knowledge of:

  • Microsoft Word
  • Excel
  • PowerPoint

Preferred knowledge of:

  • Microsoft Outlook
  • Asana

Why This Internship Is Valuable

This internship provides practical exposure to:

  • Philanthropic fundraising systems
  • Corporate and nonprofit partnerships
  • Humanitarian project support
  • Strategic communications
  • Research and benchmarking
  • International nonprofit operations

It is an excellent opportunity for students and early-career professionals seeking long-term careers in:

  • Nonprofit management
  • International development
  • Humanitarian work
  • CSR
  • Fundraising
  • Social impact consulting

How to Apply

Click here to apply

For more information about this, Click here

For more global internship opportunities Click here

The post Global Impact Project Support Internship 2026: Fully Detailed Guide to a Humanitarian and Fundraising Career Opportunity in Washington, DC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Vrije Universiteit Brussel PhD Scholarship in Sociology 2026 in Belgium: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunity on AI, Society, and Social Inequalities
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Vrije Universiteit Brussel (VUB) has officially announced applications for its highly competitive PhD Scholarship in Sociology for 2026. This exciting doctoral opportunity is designed for ambitious researchers interested in exploring the intersection of sociology, artificial intelligence, digital technologies, and social inequalities. Hosted by the Faculty of Social Sciences & Solvay Business School within the […]

The post Vrije Universiteit Brussel PhD Scholarship in Sociology 2026 in Belgium: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunity on AI, Society, and Social Inequalities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Vrije Universiteit Brussel (VUB) has officially announced applications for its highly competitive PhD Scholarship in Sociology for 2026. This exciting doctoral opportunity is designed for ambitious researchers interested in exploring the intersection of sociology, artificial intelligence, digital technologies, and social inequalities.

Hosted by the Faculty of Social Sciences & Solvay Business School within the Department of Sociology, the scholarship offers candidates the opportunity to conduct groundbreaking research while contributing to teaching activities at one of Europe’s leading research universities.

The fully funded doctoral trajectory is based in Brussels, Belgium, and provides selected candidates with financial support, extensive academic mentorship, research opportunities, teaching experience, and a dynamic international work environment.

About Vrije Universiteit Brussel (VUB)

For more than five decades, Vrije Universiteit Brussel has been recognized for its commitment to:

  • Freedom
  • Equality
  • Solidarity
  • Open inquiry
  • Critical thinking
  • Innovative research

Located in Brussels, one of Europe’s most international and politically influential cities, VUB is the largest Dutch-speaking employer in the private sector in Brussels and maintains strong international partnerships across Europe and beyond.

The university is also a proud member of EUTOPIA, an alliance of progressive European universities dedicated to transforming higher education and research collaboration.

VUB promotes:

  • Free research principles
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Socially engaged scholarship
  • Innovative academic approaches

The university strongly values diversity and welcomes applicants regardless of:

  • Gender
  • Religion
  • Skin color
  • Migration background
  • Disability
  • Neurodiversity
  • Age

About the PhD Scholarship Position

The PhD Scholarship in Sociology is offered through the Brussels Institute for Social and Population Studies within the Department of Sociology.

The doctoral trajectory spans six years and combines:

  • Research activities (approximately 70%)
  • Teaching responsibilities (approximately 30%)

This structure allows PhD researchers to gain valuable academic teaching experience while simultaneously conducting advanced doctoral research.

Research Focus: Sociology, Artificial Intelligence, and Social Inequalities

One of the most exciting aspects of this opportunity is its strong focus on the societal implications of artificial intelligence and digitalization.

The research project is situated at the intersection of:

  • Sociology
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Digital Society
  • Social Inequality
  • Data Governance
  • Computational Social Science

The selected candidate will be expected to develop an original PhD research proposal exploring how AI systems shape, reproduce, or challenge inequalities across different sectors.

Possible Research Areas Include:

  • Algorithmic bias and fairness
  • Trust in artificial intelligence
  • AI and public services
  • AI in labour markets
  • AI in education systems
  • Migration governance and digital systems
  • Data-driven public decision-making
  • Ethical AI implementation
  • Responsible AI for socio-political challenges
  • Social implications of digital technologies

The department welcomes:

  • Quantitative approaches
  • Computational methods
  • Mixed-methods research designs

Applicants with strong analytical and quantitative interests are particularly encouraged to apply.

Supervision and Research Environment

The selected PhD candidate will work under the supervision of: Tuba Bircan

The research will be conducted within a vibrant academic environment focused on:

  • Digitalization and society
  • AI and governance
  • Quantitative social science
  • Data and inequality studies

Candidates will also receive mentorship and support when preparing an application to the Research Foundation – Flanders (FWO) for a “Fellowship Fundamental Research.”

Teaching Responsibilities

The teaching component represents approximately 30% of the workload.

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting statistical and quantitative sociology courses
  • Assisting within the Bachelor of Sociology program
  • Supervising students
  • Participating in departmental activities

Because the program is taught in Dutch, applicants must have strong Dutch language proficiency at the beginning of the appointment.

Scholarship Duration and Funding

The selected candidate will receive:

  • A full-time PhD scholarship
  • Initial appointment for 12 months
  • Extension possibility up to 48 months based on positive evaluation
  • Additional extension possibility for 24 months under a different employment status

Planned Start Date

15 September 2026 

The scholarship funding follows government salary scales and provides substantial financial support for doctoral studies.

Additional Benefits at VUB

VUB offers a wide range of employee and researcher benefits designed to support well-being, work-life balance, and professional development.

Benefits Include:

1. Flexible Working Conditions

  • Extensive homeworking options
  • Telework allowance of €50 per month
  • Internet allowance of €20 per month

2. Work-Life Balance

  • 35 days of annual leave
  • Additional leave days
  • Holiday closure between Christmas and New Year

3. Healthcare and Insurance

  • Free hospitalization insurance

4. Transportation Support

  • Full reimbursement of public transport commuting costs
  • Bicycle commuting compensation

5. Campus Facilities

  • Affordable campus meals
  • Sports and fitness facilities
  • Nursery services near campus

6. Discounts and Welfare Benefits

  • Ecocheques
  • Discounts through Benefits@Work
  • Discounts on travel, shopping, amusement parks, and more

7. Professional Development

  • Access to VUB learning platforms
  • Training opportunities
  • Career development support

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Educational Qualifications

Candidates must hold a Master’s degree in:

  • Sociology
  • Statistics
  • Demography
  • Economics
  • Data Science
  • Political Science
  • Communication Science
  • Computational Social Science
  • Related social science disciplines

Language Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Excellent Dutch proficiency
  • Strong English proficiency

Candidates should be comfortable:

  • Teaching in Dutch
  • Conducting academic communication in Dutch and English
  • Writing academic materials in both languages

Research Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong quantitative mindset
  • Interest in statistical analysis
  • Curiosity about AI and society
  • Motivation for scientific research

Prior scientific research experience is considered an advantage but is not mandatory.

Additional Conditions

Applicants:

  • Must not have held an assistant mandate funded by operational resources for more than 12 cumulative months
  • Must meet Belgian residence and permit requirements if they are non-EEA nationals

Application Documents Required

Applicants must upload:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Motivation letter
  3. Diploma and academic transcripts
  4. A one-page outline of a proposed research topic relevant to the position

Application Process

The recruitment process includes:

Step 1

Initial screening of application documents

Step 2

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews scheduled between:
22–26 June 2026

Application Deadline

Deadline: 15 June 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and carefully prepare their research proposal outline to improve their chances of selection.

Submit Your Application:

The application deadline is 15 June 2026.

Click Here to Apply

Why This PhD Opportunity Stands Out
