The VUB PhD Scholarship in Sociology offers a rare opportunity for researchers to work on one of the most important issues shaping modern societies: the relationship between artificial intelligence and social inequality.

This scholarship is ideal for candidates who want to:

  • Build a strong academic career
  • Conduct socially impactful AI research
  • Gain international teaching experience
  • Work within an interdisciplinary research environment
  • Contribute to ethical and responsible AI development

For aspiring social scientists interested in the future of technology and society, this doctoral opportunity provides both academic excellence and meaningful societal impact.

For more information, visit the official website

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Vrije Universiteit Brussel PhD Scholarship in Sociology 2026 in Belgium: Fully Funded Doctoral Opportunity on AI, Society, and Social Inequalities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Research Assistant Vacancy at UNU-WIDER SA-TIED (Southern Africa – Towards Inclusive Economic Development): Big Data, Administrative Tax Data & Econometrics Opportunity in Pretoria, South Africa (2026 Intake)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER), in collaboration with the National Treasury of South Africa and the South African Revenue Service (SARS) under the SA-TIED (Southern Africa – Towards Inclusive Economic Development) initiative, is inviting applications for the position of Research Assistant (Administrative Tax Data | Big Data). This opportunity […]

The post Research Assistant Vacancy at UNU-WIDER SA-TIED (Southern Africa – Towards Inclusive Economic Development): Big Data, Administrative Tax Data & Econometrics Opportunity in Pretoria, South Africa (2026 Intake) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER), in collaboration with the National Treasury of South Africa and the South African Revenue Service (SARS) under the SA-TIED (Southern Africa – Towards Inclusive Economic Development) initiative, is inviting applications for the position of Research Assistant (Administrative Tax Data | Big Data).

This opportunity is centered on one of the most advanced data-driven research environments in Africa. South Africa is among a few countries globally that provide access to anonymized administrative tax microdata for research purposes, enabling groundbreaking work in development economics, labour markets, inequality, macro-fiscal analysis, and enterprise development.

The selected research assistant will contribute directly to building, maintaining, and improving high-value tax datasets used for policy-relevant research at the National Treasury – Secure Data Facility (NT-SDF) in Pretoria.

About the Role

The Research Assistant position is designed for highly skilled graduates in economics, econometrics, and data science-related fields. The role focuses on supporting big data infrastructure and research activities within a secure data environment.

You will work closely with the Data Developer at the NT-SDF and support both technical and research-oriented tasks involving administrative tax data.

Key Responsibilities

The selected candidate will be responsible for a wide range of technical, analytical, and administrative duties, including:

1. Data Management and Updates

  • Regularly update tax datasets stored at the National Treasury Secure Data Facility
  • Improve dataset structure, usability, and coverage
  • Expand existing datasets for enhanced research applications

2. Data Quality Assurance and Analysis

  • Conduct quality assurance checks on administrative tax datasets
  • Analyze emerging trends and inconsistencies in data
  • Ensure data integrity for research use

3. Documentation and Research Support

  • Assist in drafting, revising, and maintaining technical documentation
  • Support research teams with literature reviews and analytical write-ups
  • Help prepare research outputs based on administrative data

4. Data Security and Output Review

  • Review researcher outputs to ensure strict data confidentiality compliance
  • Verify that outputs meet Secure Data Facility regulations

5. Operational Support

  • Assist in daily operations of the National Treasury Secure Data Facility
  • Respond to data-related queries from researchers
  • Support supervisors in administrative and technical tasks

Required Qualifications and Skills

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Academic Requirements

  • Master’s degree in Economics or Econometrics
  • Candidates near completion of their Master’s degree may also be considered

Technical Skills

  • Strong proficiency in STATA, including:
    • Loops
    • Global macros
    • Directory management
    • Writing STATA programs
  • Knowledge of any of the following is an added advantage:
    • SQL
    • R
    • Python
    • ArcGIS

Professional Skills

  • Strong analytical skills
  • Excellent academic writing and drafting ability
  • Fluent written and spoken English

Eligibility Requirement

  • South African nationality OR valid residence/work permit in South Africa

Contract Terms and Compensation

  • Position type: Consultancy contract under UNU-WIDER
  • Daily stipend: ZAR 2,204 per day
  • Location: Pretoria, South Africa (National Treasury Secure Data Facility)
  • No additional housing or living allowances provided
  • Project-related expenses covered by the project

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following:

  1. Personal statement (maximum 1 page)
  2. Curriculum Vitae (maximum 2 pages)
  3. Academic transcript/record
  4. Sample STATA code (maximum 200 lines) demonstrating technical ability

Selection Process

  • Applications reviewed based on experience and motivation
  • Shortlisted candidates may undergo:
    • Technical interview
    • Competency assessment exercise
  • Only successful candidates will receive final communication

Important Dates

  • Application deadline: 9 June 2026 (23:59 GMT +2)
  • Expected start date: 30 June 2026 or soon thereafter
  • Interviews conducted shortly after shortlisting

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Research Assistant Vacancy at UNU-WIDER SA-TIED (Southern Africa – Towards Inclusive Economic Development): Big Data, Administrative Tax Data & Econometrics Opportunity in Pretoria, South Africa (2026 Intake) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
